mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window with Rank IC greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0% threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45% across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, an accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns specifically during post-shock recovery phases, with a negative Rank IC over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates signal strong aggregate demand that supports near-term corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a five-day trading window.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant positive correlation with 5-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation exceeds 0.4%, it forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 60-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal shows zero predictive power during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a negative information coefficient over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a two-month consecutive decline in industrial production signals supply-side exhaustion and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the signal loses significance outside late-cycle regimes.
